channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: PS6 : Sony rachète une technologie d’IA pour encore plus de photo-réalisme
item.description: Après le PSSR, après Project Amethyst avec AMD, Sony rachète un spécialiste de la photo-vers-3D. La machine à réalisme tourne à plein régime.

item.title: ‘Maul: Shadow Lord’ Is a Fine Prequel to a Great TV Show
item.description: The latest 'Star Wars' animated series plays with some interesting ideas, but struggles to do more than gesture to them with its slow-burn pacing.

item.title: Can AI responses be influenced? The SEO industry is trying
item.description: Let's pretend you work in IT and you're looking for a new digital service desk platform to help your employees reset passwords or onboard new hires. You use Google's AI Mode to search for suggestions, which quickly spits out a detailed answer listing companies to explore, their pricing, and what each option is best for. […]

item.title: Why AI adoption isn’t just a tech problem, but a retention risk
item.description: Are tech companies overlooking employee concerns about AI integration?

item.title: Uffizi galleries confirms it was hit by cyberattack — but claims nothing was stolen
item.description: The attackers reached out to the Uffizi director via phone to demand payment.

item.title: Robot Mowers Are Actually Good Now
item.description: With the rise of affordable and effective robot mowers, maintaining a pristine lawn has never been easier.

item.title: Time for an upgrade? Report warns outdated operating systems could be the 'unnecessary risk' your business forgot about
item.description: Outdated apps and operating systems pose serious risks - but all you have to do is install that software update.

item.title: Building private AI: control, compliance and competitive edge
item.description: Organizations want to create value with AI, but need to ensure control over their data.

item.title: Xiaomi 17 Max : un « petit » nouveau qui s’ajoutera à la longue liste des Xiaomi 17 ?
item.description: Le Xiaomi 17 Max se précise. Selon les dernières fuites, le smartphone haut de gamme de Xiaomi serait attendu en Chine dès mai 2026, avec une batterie de 8000 mAh, un capteur principal de 200 mégapixels et la puce Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 au programme.

item.title: Et si les ondes gravitationnelles du Big Bang avaient créé la matière noire ?
item.description: La matière noire reste énigmatique, mais ce n'est plus le cas des ondes gravitationnelles. Et s'il existait un lien entre les deux ? C'est ce que proposent pour la première fois deux astrophysiciens des particules.

item.title: Pourquoi « la France est sans conteste le marché stratégique » en Europe
item.description: Le géant chinois Tecno nous explique pourquoi la France est un marché hautement stratégique en Europe.

item.title: Apple App Store Experiences Surge in New Apps Amid Vibe Coding Boom
item.description: After years of gradual declines, the App Store finally saw a huge jump in new apps this past year.

item.title: Snake Bros Keep Getting Bitten by Their Lethal Pets. Only Zoos Can Save Them
item.description: Your venomous serpent bites you, and the clock is ticking. America’s zookeepers—and a cooler full of rare antivenom—are your best chance of survival.

item.title: Marshall is taking on JBL and other party speaker makers in a 'very homogenous' market — and explains why it wants to make models that 'trigger your senses'
item.description: Marshall tells me about its decision to enter into the party speaker segment, and how it's attempting to stand out from the crowd.

item.title: 'JBL-beating': our favorite 5-star cheap Bluetooth speaker just got a new model with double the battery life
item.description: Tribit has unveiled the StormBox Micro 3, with a range of upgrades over a top-rated compact speaker.

item.title: What is the release date for The Boys season 5 episodes 1 to 2 on Prime Video?
item.description: The Boys season 5 will bring down the curtain on the hit Prime Video show — here's when its two-episode premiere will drop.

item.title: It’s way too easy to cheat now
item.description: People can now trick insurance with AI-generated x-rays. It’s just the latest example of how people can scam everyone from food delivery services to the IRS. It’s so easy to cheat now. Using generative AI, anyone can get a free meal or product. They can even get free money by scamming the government itself. And, like radiologists have just discovered, they can even cheat doctors and insurance companies by using AI-generated X-rays....

item.title: How Kelly Wearstler designed her new H&M collection
item.description: The star designer talks to Fast Company about how she brought her luxe aesthetic to a mass-market audience. Swedish retailer H&M is breaking into Milan Design Week with a new collection in collaboration with the award-winning interior designer Kelly Wearstler.

item.title: The workers secretly influencing their companies’ AI usage
item.description: Call it trickle-up AI: Meet the workers taking it upon themselves to experiment with the workforce-upending technology. And their C-suites are noticing. (In a good way.) When Estefania Angel started working as an executive assistant at a large tech company a few months ago, she noticed something counterintuitive: while her company’s job was to help other enterprises set up AI to streamline their in-house tasks, her company didn’t use those systems internally itself....

item.title: Why women leaders are ditching the old workplace rulebook—and winning because of it
item.description: Forging a new model leads to happier work lives and a better bottom line. For many years, women have been told that they needed to “step-up” to lead. You know the narrative—speak more assertively, be less emotional, less sensitive and toughen up. In essence, to “fit the mold.”

item.title: Fin de Samsung Messages à partir de juillet 2026 : Google Messages s’impose sur les smartphones Galaxy
item.description: Samsung officialise la fin de son application de messagerie native. Après des années de cohabitation avec Google Messages sur les appareils Galaxy, Samsung Messages disparaîtra définitivement en juillet 2026.

item.title: Samsung Messages is officially shutting down for good — here's what you need to know
item.description: Samsung has announced that its Samsung Messages app is shutting, with Google Messages the replacement.

item.title: Report Says Software Engineer Job Listings Are Up 30% This Year
item.description: Is it the fabled “vibe coding effect”?

item.title: 5 Mysteries That the Artemis Missions to the Moon Could Finally Solve
item.description: The moon is not just a barren rock orbiting the Earth. The Artemis missions could answer the great unknowns that the satellite holds.

item.title: Pour moins de 400 €, le tout récent picoprojecteur Formovie Xming Page Two affiche des images lumineuses en Full HD
item.description: Le récent Formovie Xming Page Two, qui a succédé au très bon Xming Page One, promet de belles prestations, comme son aîné. Et il est déjà en promotion sur Amazon : il y est affiché à 369 euros au lieu de 399 euros.

item.title: 'Skipping a beat on resilience investment isn’t an option any more': IT incidents can cost firms huge amounts - here's how to stay on top of issues
item.description: Companies with strong revenue growth are more likely to invest in resilience – a balanced mix of humans and AI could hold the key.

item.title: Xiaomi Bluetooth Remote 2 Pro : une télécommande connectée avec NFC et charge USB-C
item.description: Xiaomi vient de lancer en Chine sa Bluetooth Remote 2 Pro, une télécommande pensée pour ses téléviseurs et boîtiers TV. Principal argument de vente mis en avant par la marque : le casting d'écran par simple contact NFC.

item.title: How to manage the employees that don’t clock in
item.description: AI agents are joining the workforce, so how should organizations manage and secure them?
